results.title: Stop saying “I’m fine” + 6 Alternatives - Learn English with Camille
results.content:
### Try one of these positive alternatives instead.

1 – I’m great! or I’ve been pretty great, actually.

2 – Doing really well.

3 – Couldn’t be better.

4 – Never better.

### Try one of these neutral alternatives if you aren’t “fine”

1 – I’m not too shabby, thanks.

2 – I’m not too bad, thanks.

3 – I’m pretty good.

4 – I’m okay, thanks.

### And if you REALLY want to be dramatically positive….

5 – I’m on top of the world.

6 – I’m on cloud 9.

## Join my English Success Community

I send weekly emails with “bite-sized” (small) English Lessons + Get a 10% Coupon!

### What can you say if you’re NOT fine?

1 – I’m not doing well.

2 – I’ve seen better days.

3 – Today was rough.

4 – I’m struggling a little right now.

5 – I’ve been better, to be honest. [...] ##### INTERMEDIATE PRONUNCIATION

Text reads "31 Day English Dialogue Workshop" against a blue background. A smiling person is partially visible on the right.

#### English Dialogues Workshop

## View all courses

# Stop saying “I’m fine” + 6 Alternatives

Picture of Camille Hanson

### Camille Hanson

## How are you doing today?

Did you respond with, “I’m fine” but are you tired of saying this in English?

#### Look at this dialogue:

Your friend: “How are you doing?”

You: “I’m fine.”

Your friend: “Oh no what’s wrong?”

Typically when you tell someone “I’m fine” as a response to how you are, they may think that something is wrong, that you’re actually not fine. So what can you say?

### Try one of these positive alternatives instead. [...] ### My friend Rogelio (Mexican) speaking American English

results.title: "fine" What other words do you have other than "I'm fine"? | HiNative
results.content:
Quality Point(s): 258

Answer: 65

Like: 43

## Fine can mean “good” or “okay.” It can be used to describe a feeling or another object. For example: I am fine. <—- This describes a feeling. The food was fine. <—- This describes an attribute, which was implied. There are also slang uses for the word, if you care for those. As slang, fine can mean “attractive” in a very casual manner. I would not recommend saying this to someone as it can be seen as offensive sometimes, but it is common to see it. Here’s an example: Did you see that person? They’re fine.

mongasama

## in addition to the 'fine' examples our friend @shockblack has already posted, here are a few more: fine (adjective) high quality: • a fine china tea set • made only with the finest quality butter thin, delicate: • my hair is so fine it's impossible to curl. • a fine point pen • to walk a fine line ​between life or death. tiny, powdery: • fine dust particles float in the air perceptive, sharp, discerning: • he has a fine eye for high quality antiques fine (noun) penalty, fee • I had to pay a fine for public drunkenness. ​fine (verb) having to pay a penalty, fee • I was fined $500 for public drunkenness.

results.title: Fine - Definition, Meaning & Synonyms
results.content:
Vocabulary.com

# fine

Other forms: finest; finer; fined; fines; fining

A fine is a fee you pay when you break the rules, as in not turning in library materials on time, resulting in a fine. As an adjective, fine means "high quality" or "unblemished" like fine china.

If you read music, you know that fine is a spot where the music finishes. Wine, food or porcelain that is exceptionally delicious, well-made, or beautiful is called fine. Fine also means that things are okay or acceptable, like when someone asks how you are and you reply, “I'm fine.” When you are caught doing something wrong, paying a fine, money paid as a penalty, may result.

### Vocabulary lists containing fine [...] French-English dictionary, translator, and learning
